---
title: "Positioning Dashboard"
subtitle: "Decision 2 — CRM vs Revenue Operations Platform vs AI Readiness Platform"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Dashboards <span class="sep">›</span> Positioning
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**Supports** [Decision 2 · Positioning](../decisions/positioning.qmd) — CRM, Revenue Operations, or AI
Readiness Platform?
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-grade-c">Confidence C · Illustrative</span>
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">Synthetic analyst assessment</span>
<span class="pc-next">Related: [Positioning Canvas](../frameworks/index.qmd) · [Claims &amp; Confidence](../evidence/claims-confidence.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

> **Illustrative, not validated.** The per-option scores below are an initial, *synthetic* assessment
> against the [Positioning Canvas](../frameworks/index.qmd). They structure the debate; they do not
> settle it. The `evidence_strength` criterion keeps aspirational framing honest — a position that
> sounds exciting but has no evidence is penalised.

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Imports and project path bootstrap"
import sys, pathlib
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from IPython.display import HTML

from src.utils import load_framework, weights_from_framework
from src.scoring import weighted_score

PLOTLY_TEMPLATE = "plotly_white"
OPTION_COLORS = {
    "CRM Platform": "#6b7684",
    "Revenue Operations Platform": "#2f6bb0",
    "AI Readiness Platform": "#7a4fb0",
}
```

```{python}
#| label: assessment
#| code-summary: "Analyst assessment of each option (synthetic, 0–10 per criterion)"
# Rows = positioning options; columns = the Positioning Canvas criteria (each 0–10).
# These are an initial analyst judgement to be revised as evidence accrues.
assessment = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        # option,                       clarity, diff, pain, investor, scale, evidence
        ["CRM Platform",                     9,    4,     7,       5,     6,     6],
        ["Revenue Operations Platform",      6,    7,     8,       7,     8,     5],
        ["AI Readiness Platform",            5,    8,     6,       9,     8,     3],
    ],
    columns=[
        "option", "clarity", "differentiation", "customer_pain_alignment",
        "investor_appeal", "scalability", "evidence_strength",
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: score
#| code-summary: "Apply the Positioning Canvas weights to each option"
framework = load_framework("positioning_canvas")
weights = weights_from_framework(framework)

assessment["positioning_score"] = assessment.apply(
    lambda row: weighted_score(row, weights), axis=1
)
ranked = assessment.sort_values("positioning_score", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
ranked.insert(0, "rank", ranked.index + 1)
```

```{python}
#| label: metrics
#| code-summary: "Build summary metrics (computed, not hardcoded)"
leader = ranked.iloc[0]
second = ranked.iloc[1]
margin = leader["positioning_score"] - second["positioning_score"]
weakest_evidence = assessment.sort_values("evidence_strength").iloc[0]

def metric(label, value, sub=""):
    sub_html = f'<div class="metric-sub">{sub}</div>' if sub else ""
    return (f'<div class="metric"><div class="metric-label">{label}</div>'
            f'<div class="metric-value">{value}</div>{sub_html}</div>')

HTML(
    '<div class="metric-row">'
    + metric("Options compared", len(ranked), "candidate positionings")
    + metric("Leading framing", leader["option"], f'score {leader["positioning_score"]:.1f}')
    + metric("Margin over #2", f'+{margin:.1f}', f'vs {second["option"]}')
    + metric("Weakest evidence", weakest_evidence["option"], "least-evidenced framing")
    + "</div>"
)
```

## Ranking

```{python}
#| label: table
ranked[[
    "rank", "option", "positioning_score",
    "clarity", "differentiation", "customer_pain_alignment",
    "investor_appeal", "scalability", "evidence_strength",
]]
```

```{python}
#| label: bar
fig = px.bar(
    ranked.sort_values("positioning_score"),
    x="positioning_score",
    y="option",
    orientation="h",
    color="option",
    color_discrete_map=OPTION_COLORS,
    range_x=[0, 100],
    labels={"positioning_score": "Positioning score (0–100)", "option": ""},
    template=PLOTLY_TEMPLATE,
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=300, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=20, b=10),
                  font=dict(family="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif", size=13))
fig.show()
```

## Criterion-by-criterion profile

```{python}
#| label: radar
criteria = ["clarity", "differentiation", "customer_pain_alignment",
            "investor_appeal", "scalability", "evidence_strength"]
fig = go.Figure()
for _, row in assessment.iterrows():
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatterpolar(
        r=[row[c] for c in criteria] + [row[criteria[0]]],
        theta=criteria + [criteria[0]],
        fill="toself",
        name=row["option"],
        line=dict(color=OPTION_COLORS.get(row["option"])),
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    polar=dict(radialaxis=dict(visible=True, range=[0, 10])),
    height=460, margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=30, b=40),
    template=PLOTLY_TEMPLATE,
    font=dict(family="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif", size=13),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.15, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
)
fig.show()
```

## Weights applied

```{python}
#| label: weights
pd.DataFrame(framework["criteria"])[["name", "weight", "direction", "rationale"]]
```

## Interpretation (provisional)

- **CRM Platform** wins on *clarity* but is weak on *differentiation* — buyers get it instantly, yet
  see it as commoditised (cf. evidence `EV-007`).
- **Revenue Operations Platform** balances differentiation, pain alignment, and scalability.
- **AI Readiness Platform** scores highest on *investor appeal* but lowest on *evidence strength* —
  the most exciting story is the least evidenced today (cf. Hypothesis H-CRM-AI).

::: {.callout-warning appearance="simple"}
## This is a framing tool, not a verdict
The scores are one analyst's synthetic judgement. Positioning should be decided against **customer
evidence** — message testing, win/loss analysis, discovery calls — not an internal scoring exercise.
See [Working Paper Ch. 4](../working-paper/04-crm-positioning.qmd).
:::
